pi_items.source: Task description specifies 2,000 lbs chlorine in proposed inventory
OSHA PSM Appendix A (29 CFR 1910.119 App A) - Chlorine threshold quantity confirmed from multiple official sources
EPA Risk Management Program (40 CFR 68.130) - Chlorine threshold quantity confirmed
Logical: 2000 >= 1500, therefore triggered
Logical: 2000 < 2500, therefore not triggered
Task description specifies 3,000 lbs sulfur dioxide in proposed inventory
OSHA PSM Appendix A - Sulfur Dioxide (liquid) threshold quantity confirmed
EPA Risk Management Program (40 CFR 68.130) - Sulfur Dioxide threshold quantity confirmed
Logical: 3000 >= 1000, therefore triggered
Logical: 3000 < 5000, therefore not triggered
Task description specifies 800 lbs hydrogen fluoride in proposed inventory
OSHA PSM Appendix A - Hydrogen Fluoride threshold quantity confirmed
EPA Risk Management Program (40 CFR 68.130) - Hydrogen Fluoride (anhydrous) threshold quantity confirmed at 1,000 pounds
Logical: 800 < 1000, therefore not triggered
Logical: 800 < 1000, therefore not triggered
Task description specifies 5,000 lbs methomyl in proposed inventory
Methomyl is NOT listed in OSHA PSM Appendix A (29 CFR 1910.119 App A). Confirmed by direct inspection of OSHA's official regulation page.
Methomyl is NOT listed on EPA's Risk Management Program (RMP) regulated substances list (40 CFR 68.130). Confirmed by EPA's official RMP list.
Methomyl is not regulated under OSHA PSM, therefore cannot trigger requirement
Methomyl is not regulated under EPA RMP, therefore cannot trigger requirement
Task description specifies 12,000 lbs ammonia, anhydrous in proposed inventory
OSHA PSM Appendix A - Anhydrous ammonia threshold quantity confirmed at 10,000 pounds
EPA Risk Management Program (40 CFR 68.130) - Anhydrous ammonia threshold quantity confirmed at 10,000 pounds
Logical: 12000 >= 10000, therefore triggered
Logical: 12000 >= 10000, therefore triggered

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims in task.json have been independently verified against authoritative regulatory sources. Chlorine triggers OSHA PSM (2,000 lbs >= 1,500 lbs TQ) but not EPA RMP (2,000 lbs < 2,500 lbs TQ). Sulfur Dioxide triggers OSHA PSM (3,000 lbs >= 1,000 lbs TQ) but not EPA RMP (3,000 lbs < 5,000 lbs TQ). Hydrogen Fluoride triggers neither regulation (800 lbs below both 1,000 lbs thresholds). Methomyl does not appear on either OSHA PSM Appendix A or EPA RMP regulated substances lists, and therefore cannot trigger either regulation regardless of quantity. Ammonia (anhydrous) triggers both OSHA PSM and EPA RMP (12,000 lbs >= 10,000 lbs TQ for both). All inventory amounts, threshold quantities, and triggered status determinations are correct.
